response_text:
What follows is a composite profile, drawn from common conditions described by public defenders in midsize American cities.

By 8:30 on a weekday morning, Elena Ramirez has already had three conversations that could change someone’s life. One client wants to know whether taking a plea will keep him out of jail long enough to hold onto his warehouse job. Another has missed a court date and is panicking about a warrant.
